div.h3: Intel x86-64 Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) Invalidation Architecture

div.p: Technical Note: Process Context Identifiers (PCIDs) on modern x86_64 can prevent TLB flushes on CR3 reload by tagging entries with a 12-bit ID. CR3 Bit 63 (PCID NOFLUSH) allows updating the address space without invalidation.

ul.li: Function: Flushes the translation for a single 4KB/2MB/1GB page.
Usage: Granular PTE updates without destroying entire cache state.
Function: Reloads Page Directory Base Register.
Side-effect: Flushes all TLB entries EXCEPT those with G (Global) bit.

text.tspan: * Invalidates TLB for specific virtual address

g.text: static inline void tlb_flush_single(unsigned long addr) {

text.tspan: * Invalidates TLB for specific virtual address

div.p: ✓ vmm_map_page() completion ✓ vmm_unmap_page() completion ✓ COW (Copy-On-Write) fault resolution ✓ Page permission upgrades/downgrades ✓ Single address space remapping
✓ Process Context Switching (Scheduler) ✓ Address Space (ASID) exhaustion ✓ Bulk memory remapping (paging init) ✓ Kernel-wide mapping updates ✓ Security: Speculative execution mitigation
